summary: Register the released all-k weighted multiquadratic symmetric-monodromy paper and its Kummer-wreath interface.

artifacts.title: Generic Symmetric Monodromy of Weighted Multiquadratic Sums
artifacts.summary: A self-contained all-k proof for arbitrary fixed nonzero weights: exact degree, genus and ramification, generic full symmetric monodromy, the equal-weight DBP specialization, and an exact conditional Kummer-wreath lifting criterion.

claims.statement: For every k at least 3 and every fixed weight vector with all entries nonzero over a characteristic-zero field, a nonempty Zariski-open parameter locus exists on which the weighted multiquadratic radical-sum map has geometric and generic arithmetic monodromy S_d, where d is the exact nonvanishing signed pole count.
hypotheses: The parameters a_i are pairwise distinct and lie in the stated generic boundary-good locus.
claims.scope: Weighted multiquadratic covers and their generic parameter fibers.

claims.statement: The source curve has genus 1 + 2^(k-2)(k-3), and the ramification divisor of the weighted radical-sum map has degree 2^(k-1)(k-3) + 2d.
hypotheses: The parameter lies in the boundary-good locus.
claims.scope: Exact geometry of the weighted multiquadratic source and map.

claims.statement: For equal weights, d equals delta_k = 2^(k-1) for odd k and 2^(k-1) - one half binomial(k,k/2) for even k, and the generic DBP axial mass-norm group is S_delta_k for every k at least 3.
hypotheses: The charge parameters are algebraically independent in the generic arithmetic statement.
claims.scope: All-k equal-weight DBP axial mass-norm specialization.

claims.statement: For a finite separable sheet field in characteristic different from two, the conjugate Kummer extension has kernel dual to the conjugate square-class module; full column valuation-parity rank sd is sufficient for the exact maximal wreath product C_2^s wr G.
hypotheses: The sheet field is finite separable.
For maximality, the sd conjugate square classes are independent; full column valuation-parity rank sd is one sufficient certificate.
claims.scope: Standalone conjugate Kummer modules and their application above the symmetric base cover.

relations.basis: The paper replaces the stale theorem note as proof authority, repairs its primitive-element argument, and strengthens equal weights to arbitrary fixed nonzero weights.
The paper supplies the arbitrary-k weighted base-monodromy theorem beyond Paper IV's k=4,5 scope.
The paper reconstructs and strengthens the generic Morse/value-separation argument through a finite relative ramification scheme.
The paper restates the general Kummer-wreath lift with exact base-field, rank, action-convention and necessity-versus-sufficiency boundaries.

declared_impacts.note: Use the new paper as the complete weighted/all-k proof authority; retain the note as historical provenance.
The stale-note splice is no longer needed to obtain a complete all-k proof source, though the historical note itself remains unedited.
The arbitrary-k base theorem now exists as a released corpus paper; integration into Paper IV remains editorial work.
The symmetric base and general Kummer criterion are now consolidated; the concrete crown square-class rank computation remains open.

llm.one_sentence_purpose: Provide the reusable all-k weighted symmetric-monodromy theorem and the exact Kummer-wreath interface for future DBP and arbitrary multiquadratic-cover work.
retrieval_questions: What is the generic monodromy group of a weighted sum of square roots?
What is the exact degree of the all-k DBP axial mass-norm polynomial?
How are generic simple ramification and critical-value separation proved?
What must be checked to lift an S_d base to a maximal Kummer wreath product?

extensions.verification: symbolic formulas k=3..10; four norm-degree cases; three exact exceptional configurations; 2304 semidirect cocycle cases
